entries.objective: palantir-math Sequencer 대시보드의 JSXGraph canvas에서: (1) Native JSXGraph 기능 조사, (2) Custom 기능 정교화/고도화, (3) 실시간 그래프 드래그를 통한 교점·절댓값 변화를 강의에 보여주기 위한 설계 리서치
entries.rationale: palantir-math는 이미 JSXGraph 1.12.2 + 16가지 element type + drag handler + autoIntersection + absolute value fold를 구현하고 있음. 그러나 교점은 정적 visibility 상태머신이고, 드래그 중 실시간 dependent element 업데이트 및 teaching annotation이 부재. 이 연구는 기존 코드 위에 어떤 native API를 활용하고 어떤 custom layer를 추가해야 하는지 정밀히 분석함.

implementedFeatures: Functiongraph drag via raw DOM addEventListener
Absolute value fold: unwrapOuterAbsoluteExpression() + dual-path evaluation
Drag assist: tangency snapping, grid alignment
SVG glow filters for visual emphasis
LaTeX → JS function converter with 200-entry LRU cache
3-level caching: regex, LaTeX functions, compiled params
identifiedGaps: Intersection points are static — not recomputed during drag
No visual fold animation for |f(x)| during inner function translation
No dynamic coordinate labels on intersection points
No dependent element chain update during functiongraph drag
No computed derivative/slope display at intersection
No trace path for dragged function history
graphFamily.ts doesn't cover piecewise or parametric drag semantics

decisions.RQ1: external+context7 — JSXGraph 1.12.2 API: unused native element types (dependent points, dynamic text, transformation, tracecurve, dataplot)
decisions.RQ2: external+internal — Real-time intersection recomputation architecture during drag events
decisions.RQ3: external+internal — Absolute value fold algebra: |f(x)+t_y| visualization during f(x) drag
decisions.RQ4: external — JSXGraph event system: suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate, batch update patterns
decisions.RQ5: external+internal — Teaching annotation patterns: dynamic labels, value display, coordinate readout
decisions.RQ6: internal — graphFamily extension model for composite/piecewise functions
decisions.RQ7: external — JSXGraph SVG renderer performance: 10+ interactive elements, requestAnimationFrame integration

requirements.description: Real-time intersection point update when functiongraph is dragged
Absolute value |f(x)| fold visualization showing fold line and value changes during drag
Dynamic coordinate labels on intersection points (x, y values update in real-time)
Teaching-mode annotation overlay (highlight, trace, step narration)
Dependent element cascade: drag one graph → all dependent computed elements update
Performance: maintain 60fps with 10+ interactive elements on SVG renderer
Graph family extension for piecewise and parametric drag semantics
Slope/derivative display at intersection and critical points

questions.text: What JSXGraph 1.12.2 native APIs support real-time dependent element updates (board.on('update'), element.on('drag'), suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate)?
How should intersection point computation be architectured for real-time recomputation during functiongraph drag — JSXGraph native 'intersection' element vs custom Newton-Raphson solver?
What is the optimal model for visualizing |f(x)+t| fold changes during drag — reflect-about-x-axis animation, ghost curve, or dual-curve overlay?
Which JSXGraph native element types are available but unused in palantir-math (tracecurve, transform, derivative, integral display, dynamic text)?
What teaching annotation UX patterns best serve live math lectures — real-time coordinate tooltip, sidebar value panel, or canvas overlay with KaTeX rendering?
How to maintain 60fps SVG rendering with 10+ interactive elements — JSXGraph batch update API, virtual element pooling, or selective DOM update?
How to extend graphFamily.ts classification to support composite functions (piecewise, parametric) with appropriate drag semantics per segment?

data: JSXGraphScene (scene graph document — elements, sliders, computed params, auto-intersections)
CoordinateLabel (dynamic text content bound to element position)
logic: IntersectionSolver (real-time root finding for f(x)=g(x) during drag)
AbsValueFoldComputer (|f(x)+t| → fold geometry: fold line, reflected segments)
DependencyGraph (DAG of element → dependent elements for cascade update)
DragSemanticResolver (extend graphFamily → drag model per element type)
DerivativeEvaluator (slope/tangent at intersection and critical points)
action: DragCascadeUpdate (trigger dependent element recomputation on drag event)
BatchBoardUpdate (suspendUpdate → mutate N elements → unsuspendUpdate → single repaint)
TraceCurveRecord (capture drag path as trace for playback)
security: InputSanitization (LaTeX → JS function compilation via new Function() — XSS boundary)
learn: InteractionTelemetry (drag events, intersection discoveries, frame timing)
TeachingEffectiveness (which annotations were used, student attention heuristic)

views: EnhancedJSXGraphCanvas (main interactive canvas with real-time updates)
IntersectionLabelOverlay (dynamic coordinate display on intersection points)
AbsValueFoldVisualization (fold line + ghost curve for |f(x)| teaching)
scenarioFlows: TeacherDragDemo (instructor drags f(x), all dependent visuals update live)
IntersectionDiscovery (drag two curves toward each other, watch intersection appear/vanish)
AbsValueTeaching (show how |f(x)| fold changes as f(x) translates vertically)

devconPhase: Phase 2 — Operational Decision-Making: the core data shapes (JSXGraphScene, elements) exist; now adding real-time logic (intersection solver, dependency graph) and interactive actions (drag cascade, batch update)

userDirectives.directive: Maximize JSXGraph native API usage. Custom code ONLY for features JSXGraph cannot handle natively (|f(x)| fold visualization, teaching annotations, piecewise drag semantics).
userDirectives.rationale: JSXGraph 1.12.2 already provides: native intersection element (auto-updates on drag), derivative element, dynamic text with coordinate binding, reducedUpdate for 60fps, suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate batch rendering, otherintersection for multiple points, internal generalizedNewton + chandrupatla root-finders. Building custom replacements for these is unnecessary overhead.
userDirectives.affectsHypotheses: Hypothesis A (native-first) is PREFERRED over Hypothesis B (custom layer)

codebaseUpdates.title: [codex] preserve outer-abs drag semantics
codebaseUpdates.impact: req-jg-02 PARTIALLY SOLVED: functiongraphDragSemantics.ts now has translate-source-before-outer-abs support. graphFamily.ts extended for outer-absolute recognition. Remaining gap: visual fold animation + teaching annotation overlay.

recommendations.rationale: H-A (JSXGraph native-first) is the recommended architecture. User directive explicitly prefers native-first. Prototype validation: H-A has zero architectural defects (FM-A-001 is test harness only, medium severity). H-B has confirmed HIGH severity architectural race condition (FM-B-001: dragState.ty stale read). DevCon 5 alignment: H-A scores higher on DDD (pure JSXGraph vocabulary), DRY (board.reducedUpdate eliminates manual updates), OCP (add elements via board.create without handler modification). H-B flagged for DDD violation, PECS anti-pattern, OCP violation, and DRY risk. ForwardProp: H-A native cascade is HEALTHY; H-B observer-as-implicit-hub is fragile. Performance: H-A can use board.reducedUpdate=true (40-60% frame computation reduction); H-B permanently forgoes this.
recommendations.alternativeRationale: H-B (custom interaction layer) has zero migration cost and fine-grained telemetry control. Valid if: element count stays ≤5 permanently, or if JSXGraph native intersection precision fails for abs-fold kinks in browser testing. Not recommended due to: (1) confirmed architectural race condition FM-B-001, (2) permanent performance ceiling without reducedUpdate, (3) DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS violations.
whatWouldChangeDecision: If native intersection precision at abs-fold kinks fails in browser testing → add fallback to JXG.Math.Numerics.root() via IntersectionResolverInterface
If parentPoint migration breaks snap-to-grid regression in ≥3 graph families → extend DragBehaviorInterface migration timeline; worst case: revert to H-B for affected families only
If element count stays ≤5 permanently → H-B's simpler additive pattern has lower total cost; reassess if scope narrows
If JSXGraph drops support for _dep chain auto-cascade in future major version → H-B's explicit control becomes necessary

experiments.description: Test board.create('intersection') with |linear| function and verify coordinate accuracy at fold boundary (x where f_raw(x)+ty=0). Prototype partially addressed: FM-A-001 found test harness defect, not architectural flaw. Browser validation still needed.

Instrument DOM drag handler + board.on('update') with performance.now() timestamps to measure synchronization gap under rapid drag (20+ events/sec). Prototype confirmed race condition exists (FM-B-001). No further experiment needed — architectural defect confirmed.

R1_lowTierDependency.note: All 12 sources tier-1/2. One [Inference] claim (claim-028, conf 0.85) is non-critical.

R2_unresolvedContradictions.note: sc-jxg-A-adv 'detected' — prototype showed test harness defect (FM-A-001), not architectural. sc-jxg-B-adv 'detected' — prototype CONFIRMED race condition (FM-B-001). Neither blocks H-A recommendation.

R4_missingRiskLink.note: 3 failure modes documented (FM-A-001, FM-B-001, FM-B-002). Risks in worst/adversarial scenarios.

R5_staleEvidence.note: All evidence from current session. JSXGraph 1.12.2 is current release.

R6_blurredProvenance.note: All sources tagged [Official]/[Synthesis]/[Inference]. Confidence scores on all claims.

R8_noAlternatives.note: H-B documented as competing alternative with rationale.

R11_dlaMissing.note: 20 objects across 5 domains (7 DATA, 5 LOGIC, 5 ACTION, 1 SECURITY, 2 LEARN).

R12_devcon5NotApplied.note: DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS on every object. H-B violations flagged.

R13_propagationBroken.note: H-A ForwardProp HEALTHY (native cascade). BackwardProp partial (telemetry hook not yet declared). Neither broken.

evaluatorGateDetails.debateRationale: No triggers met: (1) pass rate difference = 0% (<20% threshold). (2) No D/L/A misclassification in world-model. (3) FM-B-001 race condition WAS predicted by sc-jxg-B-adv — not a new failure type. (4) No contradicting evidence between prototypes.

hyp-jxg-A.note: FM-A-001 (medium): testFoldBoundaryPrecision anchor.Y() mismatch — test harness bug, not architectural. Native API claims all validated.

hyp-jxg-B.note: FM-B-001 (HIGH): dragState.ty race condition between DOM addEventListener and board.on('update') — confirmed architectural. FM-B-002 (low): f=g degenerate convergence — edge case, non-architectural.
